behavior_summary: DEADCLOZ::look responds to an actor 'looking' at the dead man's clothes by producing the short description "dead man's clothes ".
The routine allocates or references a temporary string holding that literal, then calls Item::bark with the string and the viewer/process id to display the description to the actor.
After calling Item::bark the code frees the temporary string, captures the bark return value, and uses it in an implication/pop sequence before suspending/returning.
Surrounding opcodes set item-local info and apply a process exclusion; these appear to be local item-state or flow-control operations rather than writes to global quest flags.
notable_control_flow: Entry triggered by the 'U8P_DEADCLOZ::look' event (Event 0) for the item.
Sequence: set item info / process exclude → build description string → push viewer pid → call Item::bark → free string → capture return value → implies/pop → suspend/return.
The return value from Item::bark is captured and used in an implies-style operation, suggesting the bark result influences immediate control flow/return semantics.
open_questions: Exact semantics and side effects of the 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes in this handler — what item-local state 0x210 represents and whether it affects other scripts.
Precise meaning of the return value from Item::bark and how the subsequent 'implies' opcode affects higher-level game logic (treated as boolean flow control or procedural result).
Whether the temporary string is allocated on the heap or referenced from static data (disassembly shows allocation/free but not exact storage).
Whether there are additional conditionals (e.g., player state, visibility) that can prevent the bark from being executed (none observed here).

main_function.name: U8P_DEADCLOZ::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_DEADCLOZ::look' verb on the dead man's clothes: present a short description to the viewing actor by calling Item::bark, then process the bark result before returning.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_DEADCLOZ::look) — triggered when an actor looks at this item (the dead man's clothes).

function_map.name: U8P_DEADCLOZ::look
function_map.role: Responds to U8P_DEADCLOZ::look/examine events for the dead man's clothes; builds and emits description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine called to display/emit a short text message on behalf of an item/process.

external_calls.purpose: Send the short description string "dead man's clothes " to the viewer/process id so the actor sees the item's description.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: DEADCLOZ::look responds to an actor 'looking' at the dead man's clothes by producing the short description "dead man's clothes ".
